extracted_information: The Edera blog post provides detailed performance benchmarks and analysis of Edera Protect, a container security solution, comparing its performance against Docker, gVisor, Kata Containers, and Firecracker across various metrics including memory throughput, CPU performance, system call overhead, real-world workload (Linux kernel build), and container startup time. The analysis highlights Edera's ability to provide strong isolation with minimal performance impact, often outperforming gVisor and competitive with Kata Containers and Firecracker.

tests.name: sysbench read
tests.firecracker: omitted (doesn't support manual memory allocations), within 2% of Docker in lower memory tests

tests.name: sysbench write
tests.firecracker: omitted (doesn't support manual memory allocations), within 2% of Docker in lower memory tests

tests.name: memcpy
tests.firecracker: omitted (doesn't support manual memory allocations), within 2% of Docker in lower memory tests

tests.name: memset
tests.firecracker: omitted (doesn't support manual memory allocations), within 2% of Docker in lower memory tests

memory_throughput.analysis: Edera's virtualization maintains near-native memory performance. gVisor shows poorest performance due to its userspace kernel approach.

cpu_performance.test: sysbench prime number calculation (up to 150,000, 4 threads)
cpu_performance.firecracker: significant performance limitations (likely due to thread utilization issues)
cpu_performance.analysis: Edera's isolation adds virtually no computational overhead.

system_call_performance.edera_standard_mode: moderate overhead vs. Docker (fork is 41.8% slower)
system_call_performance.gvisor: consistently poorest performance (due to security-focused interception)
system_call_performance.kata_firecracker: perform well (leverage virtualization extensions similar to Edera's PVH mode)
system_call_performance.analysis: Edera's flexible architecture allows enabling PVH mode for critical system call performance.

real_world_performance_linux_kernel_buil.analysis: Edera introduces minimal overhead for complex, mixed workloads; minimal tradeoff for security benefits.

container_startup_time.analysis: Edera's architecture allows dynamic resource allocation and pre-provisioning to mitigate startup cost.

features.name: Edera Protect
features.description: Container-native hypervisor leveraging hardware virtualization extensions to provide strong isolation between containers while maintaining performance. Offers a 'Hardened Runtime Standard' for AI and Application Security.
key_capabilities: Memory and CPU performance comparable to or better than Docker
System call performance exceeding Docker with PVH mode
Minimal overhead (5% slower) for real-world workloads compared to Docker
Significantly outperforms gVisor and matches/exceeds Kata Containers and Firecracker in various benchmarks
Dynamic resource allocation for container pre-provisioning and scaling

features.name: gVisor
features.description: A userspace kernel providing strong isolation for containers. Its approach leads to significant performance overhead.
performance_characteristics: Poorest memory performance across all tests
Consistently poorest system call performance due to security-focused interception

features.name: Kata Containers
features.description: Hypervisor-based container solution that leverages virtualization extensions for isolation.
performance_characteristics: Follows closely behind Docker in CPU performance
Performs well in system call benchmarks (leveraging virtualization extensions)
Outperformed by Edera in real-world Linux kernel build benchmark
Slower container startup time compared to Docker and Edera

features.name: Firecracker
features.description: Virtual machine monitor designed for serverless workloads, leveraging virtualization extensions.
performance_characteristics: Omitted from memory and Linux kernel build benchmarks due to specific requirements (manual memory allocation, sufficient memory)
Within 2% of Docker in tests with lower memory requirements
Significant CPU performance limitations (likely due to thread utilization issues)
Performs well in system call benchmarks (leveraging virtualization extensions)
Slower container startup time compared to Docker and Edera

features.name: Docker
features.description: Standard container runtime, used as a baseline for 'near-native' or 'no isolation' performance.
performance_characteristics: Highest baseline performance across most benchmarks when no isolation overhead is present
